title: [v4.2.0-rhel] Partially revert 2b3867e
body:
The Fedora-36 CI VMs used prior to 2b3867e closely matched RHEL-8.6
which is the intended destination of this v4.2.0-rhel release branch.
Importantly this change, along with one or more future commits
(f34c2726..31e11a06) lead to downstream build failures on RHEL 8.6,
and reproduce using the original Fedora-36 CI VMs.  In other words,
leaving the F36 CI VMs in place would have allowed these failures
to be caught during upstream rather than downstream testing.

Also strip the F41 aarch64 build as this similarly isn't relevant
in a RHEL 8.6 context, nor was this architecture present in CI
for this branch previously.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Replace golang.org/x/crypto with forked and patched version to address CVE-2025-22869
```

comments.body:
Now we can see [the failure in F36](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5480837908529152):

```
vendor/golang.org/x/crypto/curve25519/curve25519.go:13:8: cannot find package "github.com/containers/podman/vendor/crypto/ecdh" in:
	/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/vendor/crypto/ecdh
```

This is basically identical to the error I see when trying to build this branch for RHEL 8.6.  It does not occur when building in F41, hence I'm hoping to also fix it in this PR unless someone has a better idea.

@Luap99 or @dashea did you encounter this/similar on other branches or have any hints on how to fix it?

(I tried `make vendor` but it doesn't seem to change anything)

@cevich That means the go version is to old to compile the golang/x/crypto version so you will need to downgrade even further which means it needs a new tag on the openshift crypto fork. `crypto/ecdh` was added in go 1.20 AFAICS.
I think @dashea mentioned that he has backported the change to some older crypto version on his fork but would need help to publish that on the openshift fork.
I guess worst case you have to replace it with your own fork.

Sorry about the go bump, I assumed we rebased the go version into RHEL by now. Seems that is not the case which means patching everything is so much more complicated than it should be.

> Sorry about the go bump, I assumed we rebased the go version into RHEL by now. Seems that is not the case which means patching everything is so much more complicated than it should be.

Yep, after chatting with David, it seems we're back to "the hard way" :frowning: 

Thanks @Luap99 for taking a look and confirming that's definitely the problem we're hitting.  We'll work on getting additional compatible crypto fork branches created and bring those in here (and similar PRs).
@dashea PTAL make sure I spelled and versioned everything correctly.
